# BUILDABOX.md

## Purpose

Buildabox is the build process for Owlbox artifacts.

It generates `OWLBOX.md`, `README.md`, and `VISION.md` from canonical repository files.

## Generated Files

### `OWLBOX.md`

Generated from:

- `owlbox/OUTLINE.md`
- `owlbox/WISDOM.md`
- `owlbox/LEGACY.md`

`OWLBOX.md` is the repository continuity view.

In the Owlbox repository, this generated file is the continuity view for maintaining the Owlbox tool project itself.

### `README.md`

Generated from selected publication files.

`README.md` is the short landing page for the Owlbox repository.

### `VISION.md`

Generated by concatenating the project files that describe the Owlbox system.

`VISION.md` is for reading the full Owlbox publication context in one place.

## Scripts

- `scripts/build-owlbox.ps1`: generates `OWLBOX.md`.
- `scripts/build-readme.ps1`: generates `README.md`.
- `scripts/build-vision.ps1`: generates `VISION.md`.
- `scripts/build-all.ps1`: runs the build scripts in order.

## Skill Reproduction

The Owlbox specification must be capable of reproducing the Codex Owlbox skill exactly.

The distributable skill lives at `codex/owlbox/`. Its files are part of the Owlbox repository source, and `VISION.md` includes them verbatim so the complete skill can be reviewed from the generated publication artifact.

Do not paraphrase the skill or templates when copying them. Changing exact skill or template language without an intentional spec change is Fray.

## Build Order

1. Edit canonical source files.
2. Run `scripts/build-all.ps1`.
3. Validate generated files in proportion to risk.
4. Commit source files and generated files together.

`scripts/build-owlbox.ps1` is a necessary build script because `OWLBOX.md` is generated from canonical Owlbox source files. It is not optional installer machinery.

## PowerShell Build Command

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\build-all.ps1
```

This command runs the full local build for the Owlbox repository.
